title: EUI-64 bytes logged in plaintext via LOG_INF
description:
In lora_l2.c:125-127, the full EUI-64 is logged via LOG_INF:
LOG_INF("EUI-64: %02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x:%02x", ...)

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:125-127

The EUI-64 is derived from SHA-256(hardware_id) and serves as the node's link-layer identity. While not a cryptographic secret per se, logging the full EUI-64 exposes device tracking information. Combined with the link-local address log at lichen_l2.c:417, an attacker with log access can correlate physical devices across deployments.

Impact: Device identity leaked to logs. In constrained deployments where logs are centralized or accessible to attackers, this enables device fingerprinting and tracking.

Recommendation: Either reduce log level to LOG_DBG, truncate to last 4 bytes, or remove entirely. The information is useful for debugging but should not appear in production logs.
